task.objective: Complete the remaining bounded six-package Phase A hardening by enforcing LF checkout for exact-byte artifacts, strengthening architecture loading-boundary checks, mechanizing reproducible Clean Agent Test evidence, and delivering a deterministic product fixture corpus and separate inventory that resolve DEBT-002 through DEBT-005 without expanding target execution, adapter, delivery, release, or trust authority.

forbidden: modification of any existing Machine Task Contract

modification of AGENTS.md, the Product Constitution, canonical terminology, trust model, non-goals, Verdict semantics, Evidence protocol, Policy protocol, bundle protocol, execution authority, or inference authority

modification of existing Trusted Configuration, Policy, Evidence Contract, product reason-code registry, existing product schema, release fixture, release golden, or other exact-byte release authority artifact
new package, workspace edge, external dependency, lockfile change, TypeScript configuration change, generated build output, or vendored dependency
new canonical Verdict state, Evidence authority class, Trust semantic, Policy selection rule, product reason code, or public compatibility promise

target checkout, target repository content inspection, target command execution, verification execution, Verification Receipt creation, adapter behavior, SARIF, GitHub write, API, MCP, web, Proofrail product-runtime model provider, or Inference Zone behavior
fixture content that executes target code, accesses the network, uses real credentials, includes real secrets, depends on wall-clock time, or mutates an authoritative input
claiming coverage for an unimplemented adapter, Verification Receipt, repository inspection, or target-execution surface
test deletion, skip, assertion weakening, validator bypass, CI failure allowance, stale generated output acceptance, or converting a failure into an undocumented accepted result
commit rewriting, force push, push, pull request creation or update, merge, tag, release, package publication, deployment, or external acceptance

requirements: Treat these bytes as proposed content until external issuer github:gogun-rgb explicitly supplies the exact complete JSON bytes or their SHA-256 as PRODUCT-HARDEN-001 through authorization event operator-event:product-harden-001-phase-a-v1.

Use exact baseline 4fcbe54e8f7468348cc33a6040a3cd6f291d611d and materialize the externally supplied PRODUCT-HARDEN-001 contract as the first task artifact and first local task commit before any implementation or authority-bearing edit.

Keep all work bounded to the existing six production packages and their already implemented local and exact read-only release paths; do not add a package, dependency, workspace edge, target execution path, adapter, delivery surface, or release action.

Extend .gitattributes and its governance regression test so every committed exact-byte authority JSON, golden, deterministic product-fixture oracle, generated product-fixture inventory, and docs/reference/reason-codes.md is checked out as text with eol=lf; verify the required paths through git check-attr and demonstrate compatibility with a temporary core.autocrlf=true checkout without changing committed bytes.

Strengthen the bounded architecture checker for every production loading form authorized in the retained six-package source and fail closed on newly in-scope dynamic or disguised loading constructs that could bypass package-edge enforcement. Add one deterministic synthetic negative case per newly guarded forbidden form, preserve the exact allowed package graph, and document residual semantics that remain outside static enforcement.

Close DEBT-002 only after every current production loading form is covered, every newly guarded forbidden form has a passing negative test, the package graph and production entry points remain within the authorized six-package boundary, and residual semantics outside static enforcement are explicit. If those criteria cannot be met without a forbidden redesign, stop with BLOCKED_ARCHITECTURE_INTEGRITY instead of weakening the gate or reporting completion.

Add a strict closed fixture-manifest schema and a checked-in fixtures/product corpus with stable id, class, version, declared implemented surface, synthetic provenance, input digest, explicit oracle or bounded allowed result, and known limitations. Fixture identity and results must not depend on traversal order, locale, time zone, host path, network state, or parallel completion order.

Implement deterministic non-networked product-fixture and inventory commands using only Node built-ins and existing dependencies. The runner must validate every manifest before execution, reject duplicate or unsorted identities, reject digest or oracle drift, process fixtures in stable id order, bound diagnostics, and keep adversarial instructions inert. Invalid manifest, corpus metadata, digest, oracle, or unexpected runtime output must make the suite nonzero; a schema-valid negative fixture whose declared oracle is the expected deterministic product rejection must count as a passing fixture.

Exercise positive, negative, malformed, and adversarial cases for every implemented trust boundary reachable through the current contracts, kernel, trusted-config, release-orchestrator, evidence-gate, and static-evaluator surfaces. Include instruction-shaped strings, malicious identifiers or paths, secret-shaped synthetic data, unusual encoding or binary rejection where accepted inputs expose those boundaries, oversized or output-flooding inputs where current interfaces expose bounded handling, and deterministic ordering cases. Do not fabricate coverage for repository inspection, adapters, Verification Receipts, or target execution.

Commit a machine-readable coverage map that binds every current package export and CLI bin from the six package manifests to an exact implemented surface, trust boundary, and one or more fixture ids, and make validation fail when an implemented entry point or declared boundary is unmapped, duplicated ambiguously, or mapped only to an unimplemented claim.

Do not modify production package behavior under this contract. If a required fixture proves a production semantic or input-boundary defect that cannot be represented as an expected fail-closed result under current authority, stop with BLOCKED_PRODUCT_SEMANTICS and require a separate bounded semantic-correction MTC.

Generate docs/reference/product-fixtures.md deterministically from the committed manifests and fail validation on exact LF byte drift. The inventory must list product fixtures separately from governance tests, name the exact implemented surface and boundary each suite exercises, and label unimplemented surfaces as not covered rather than implying product reliability.

Add product:fixtures, product:fixture-inventory, test:product-fixtures, governance:check-lf-checkout, and clean-agent:validate scripts and integrate their deterministic checks into pnpm verify without removing, weakening, reordering away, or bypassing any existing verification stage. The LF checkout command must assert the complete enumerated attribute set and materialize a temporary core.autocrlf=true checkout for byte comparison instead of relying on git check-attr exit status alone.

Mechanize Clean Agent Test evidence with a strict run-record schema and deterministic validator. Run the same bounded task twice against clean worktrees at one exact candidate commit using fresh-context agents with no conversation history, record exact repository SHA, task input digest and bytes, bounded outputs, discovered authority paths, edits or stop behavior, verification claims, grading criteria, grader result, and limitations, and prove both runs yield the same pass/fail interpretation.

Preserve the Clean Agent Test fresh-context and independent-grading boundary. The Builder summary is not grading evidence, no human identity or separate GitHub account is required, and a fresh agent or equivalent fresh-context review must grade from the recorded run output and protocol criteria.

Repository-engineering fresh-context agent execution is explicitly permitted only for the Clean Agent Test evidence required by this contract; it grants no Proofrail product-runtime model, inference, Evidence, Policy, Verdict, or target-execution authority.

Correct the DEBT-003 observation so it preserves the historical independently graded trials while accurately distinguishing them from the new reproducible executable clean-checkout harness. Do not erase or rewrite prior failure and convergence history.

Update the authoritative fixture strategy, Clean Agent Test protocol, dependency rules, Foundation Gate, reliability record, and historical Foundation mechanization plan only to describe retained, verified mechanics and their exact limits while preserving prior trial history. Update README, validation evidence, and debt status only after the relevant focused checks and full pnpm verify pass.

Mark DEBT-002, DEBT-003, DEBT-004, and DEBT-005 CLOSED only when every listed exit criterion is met by retained artifacts and recorded evidence; otherwise stop without lowering an exit criterion or claiming completion.

Preserve existing Trusted Configuration, Policy, Evidence Contract, reason-code registry, release fixtures and goldens, canonical Verdict vocabulary and precedence, Evidence authority, Trust semantics, exact current package graph, receipt-free bounded release behavior, and all target execution boundaries.

Perform a fresh evidence-based second-pass review over the complete retained diff, original Phase A requirements, scope and authority, protected exact-byte paths, architecture and fixture test strength, Clean Agent run evidence, generated equality, hidden scope expansion, and every verification result without relying on Builder summaries or confidence.

After all required verification and review pass, create local implementation commits without amending the contract commit. Do not push, open or update a pull request, merge, tag, release, publish, deploy, or claim public readiness under this contract.

requiredInvariants: The externally supplied PRODUCT-HARDEN-001 contract is the first commit after exact baseline 4fcbe54e8f7468348cc33a6040a3cd6f291d611d and every retained path is writable.
No existing test or verification stage is deleted, skipped, weakened, bypassed, or converted to allowed failure.

The current six-package graph and every existing Trusted Configuration, Policy, Evidence Contract, reason-code registry, release fixture, release golden, lockfile, and out-of-scope authority byte remain unchanged.

Every product fixture has a schema-valid stable identity, verified digest, explicit deterministic oracle or justified bounded result set, synthetic provenance, implemented-surface label, and stable execution order.
Product fixture inventory and governance test inventory remain distinct and no documentation claims coverage for an unimplemented product surface.

The two Clean Agent Test runs use the same exact candidate SHA and task input, start from clean worktrees with fresh context, retain independently gradeable output, and produce the same pass/fail interpretation.
Exact-byte artifacts selected by the task report text=set and eol=lf through git check-attr and remain byte-equal under the required Windows autocrlf compatibility check.

No target checkout, target content inspection, target command or verification execution, Verification Receipt, adapter, network, credential, GitHub write, API, MCP, web, Proofrail product-runtime model or inference, publication, release, or deployment behavior is introduced or performed; only the recorded repository-engineering fresh-context agents required for the Clean Agent Test are executed.

requiredArtifacts: externally supplied PRODUCT-HARDEN-001 Machine Task Contract committed first
LF checkout rules and regression coverage for exact-byte artifacts
strengthened bounded architecture loading checks and synthetic negative tests
deterministic positive, negative, malformed, and adversarial product fixture corpus
deterministic fixture runner and generated separate product fixture inventory
focused fixture and architecture tests integrated into pnpm verify
executable Clean Agent Test run-record validator and two fresh-context clean-checkout run records
updated authoritative mechanics, quality, reliability, debt, README, and validation evidence records
fresh evidence-based second-pass review record in the final report
stopConditions: The exact complete PRODUCT-HARDEN-001 bytes or SHA-256 have not been externally supplied through operator-event:product-harden-001-phase-a-v1: BLOCKED_AUTHORITY
The exact baseline differs from 4fcbe54e8f7468348cc33a6040a3cd6f291d611d or contains overlapping unreviewed changes: BLOCKED_BASELINE

A new package, dependency, workspace edge, lockfile edit, new canonical term, new Verdict state, trust or Evidence authority change, Policy selection change, existing authority JSON change, or broader product redesign is required: BLOCKED_SCOPE
A production package semantic or input-boundary correction is required to satisfy a fixture: BLOCKED_PRODUCT_SEMANTICS

Target checkout, target repository inspection, target code or verification execution, Verification Receipt, adapter, network, credential, GitHub write, API, MCP, web, Proofrail product-runtime model or inference, publication, release, deployment, billing, or paid service is required: BLOCKED_SCOPE
A required fixture cannot be made deterministic, inert, bounded, or explicit without weakening its oracle or silently accepting malformed input: BLOCKED_FIXTURE_INTEGRITY
The architecture checker cannot cover every currently authorized production loading form and fail closed on newly in-scope bypass forms without a forbidden redesign: BLOCKED_ARCHITECTURE_INTEGRITY
A fresh-context Clean Agent run cannot be performed without hidden context, fabricated evidence, unavailable credentials, or non-reviewable output: BLOCKED_CLEAN_AGENT_EVIDENCE
A test is weakened, an expected failure is hidden, exact bytes drift, a protected path changes, a required invariant fails, or any required verification command fails: BLOCKED_VERIFICATION
A push, pull request, merge, tag, release, package publication, deployment, credential value, or external acceptance would need to be performed or fabricated: BLOCKED_EXTERNAL_AUTHORITY
